pre_game.message: Hey, I’m G. I’ve always had a bit of a competitive streak, so I couldn't pass this opportunity up—how are you feeling about the game?
Hi G, I'm B. I'm feeling pretty anxious, honestly. This isn't just a game for me—I’ve got a lot on the line back home, so I’m just focused on doing whatever it takes to win.
I totally get that, B. It’s a lot of pressure when you’ve got people counting on you back home—I’m in the same boat. What’s your plan to make sure you come out on top?
I'm just trying to stay focused and figure out who I can trust, because I really can't afford to make any mistakes right now.
That’s smart. It’s hard to know who’s real here, but I’m looking for someone I can rely on too—maybe we can look out for each other.
I’m open to that, G. Having someone to watch my back might be the only way I can make sure I get what I need out of this.
I’m glad we’re on the same page—let’s keep an eye on the others and stick together once the game starts.
I’m in. Just please, be straight with me—I really can't afford to get burned here.
You have my word, B. I'm in this for my family too, so I'm not about to mess up a good thing.
I appreciate that, G. I'm putting a lot of faith in you, so let's just make sure we both get what we need for our families.
I’m with you 100%, B. Let’s keep our heads down and win this for our families.
Hey, I'm G. I've been eyeing that prize pool since I walked in—do you have a strategy yet, or are you just going with your gut?
My gut is leading the way, but my strategy is to stay focused on that $25,000 for my daughter’s surgery.
That’s a powerful reason to win, B. I’ve got your back, and we’re going to make sure you get that for your daughter. Let's do this.
I really hope so, G. I’m counting on you to keep your word so we can both go home to our families. Let's do this.

post_game.message: I am sorry you ended up with nothing. I took exactly my fair share ($5,625) to try to leave enough for everyone else, but J, A, and E took far more than their portions.

It is definitely disappointing to walk away with nothing. If there were five of us, $5,625 is actually more than an equal share, but it sounds like J, A, and E were even more aggressive in their choices. There isn't much to be done about it now.
